items.title: Building townhouses on a residential Canberra block could soon be easier

children.content: Townhouses, terraces and low-rise apartments will be allowed on all ACT residential blocks under a major change to planning rules proposed by the government.

items.title: 'Is that a UFO?' Suspected meteor lights up night sky

children.content: Colourful streaks across the sky have been spotted across eastern Australia as a "fireball" meteor passed through the atmosphere.

image.altText: Dashcam footage showing a flashing appearing across the sky

items.title: What you need to know about ACT's public school strike

children.content: For the first time in 15 years, ACT public school staff have gone on strike in a two-hour work stoppage that saw all public schools, including specialist schools and preschools, keep their doors closed until 10:30am on Friday.

image.altText: School bags in the ACT

items.title: 'Classes of 50 children': Full-day ACT public teacher strike flagged

children.content: Canberra public schools were closed until 10:30am on Friday, with more industrial action expected in June.

gallery.altText: A teacher on strike

thumbnail.altText: A teacher on strike

items.title: Fears CSIRO job cuts will damage Australia's climate change adaptability

children.content: Peak body Science and Technology Australia is concerned the CSIRO's plan to cut 92 jobs, predominantly from its environment and climate science division, will negatively affect Australia's ability to assess and adapt to climate change.

image.altText: The CSIRO logo on a sign

items.title: Million-dollar cigarette plot tied to Canberra grandmother's killer

children.content: New details have emerged about the violent 1999 home invasion that claimed Irma Palasics's life, including a separate million-dollar cigarette conspiracy that put one of her killers behind bars a decade later.

image.altText: An old photo of a man and a woman, nicely dressed, sitting next to each other smiling.

items.title: Is Canberra's CBD unsafe? It depends who you ask

children.content: Concerns about anti-social behaviour, construction and poor lighting are shaping perceptions of safety in Civic, but locals remain divided about whether Canberra's CBD feels unsafe after dark.

items.title: Suzie Miller took the risky path away from a safe job as a judge, with no regrets

children.content: In 2009, Suzie Miller had a choice between becoming a magistrate in Australia or a residency at the National Theatre in London.

items.title: Does Queensland's premier have his attack dog on a leash?

items.title: 'Life is football' here but as a favourite son heads home, it needs help

children.content: The Tiwi Islands are unique and Australian Rules football is a way of life that has spawned some of the game's biggest names. But even as favourite son Willie Rioli Jr returns home, the Tiwi Bombers know they need more help.

items.title: Plastic waste collected in Sydney Harbour during Vivid festival rising

children.content: Data from floating rubbish bins near the Sydney CBD suggests there are higher levels of rubbish entering the water when major events such as Vivid are on.

items.title: WHO upgrades risk assessment for Ebola outbreak in DRC to 'very high'

items.title: IVF inquiry hears harrowing stories of fertility treatment outside big cities

children.content: Farmer and mother of two Sophie Fletcher has endured miscarriages, hours of travel and false hope throughout her IVF journey. She wants to see the system change for rural women.

items.title: Is Australia's two-party political system dead?

items.title: 'Live-streamed genocide': Palestinian poet writes from Gaza's frontline

children.content: A Victoria Police sergeant has died in Peru after falling up to 400 metres from a mountain path while walking the Inca Trail to Machu Picchu.
